---
title: "Birth Rate Collapse and Its Effect on Interest Rates"
author: "Jason Lu and Coen Teulings"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
---

## Abstract

We study the macroeconomic consequences of the post-1970 fertility collapse
using a large overlapping-generations model with CES production calibrated to
Germany. A two-parameter fertility shock — capturing the permanent transition
from a high to a low Total Fertility Rate — drives cohort-size dynamics.
Solving for the transitional interest rate path via an iterative algorithm,
we find that real interest rates fall steeply from around 1985, trough near
2035 overshooting the new balanced growth path level, and gradually recover
thereafter.

## Introduction

## Demographic Module

### Birth Rate Equation

### Cohort Size Simulation

### Germany: Age Distribution

## OLG Model

### Households

### Firms

### Equilibrium and BGP

### Transitional Dynamics Algorithm

## Results: Germany Baseline

### Transitional Interest Rate Path

### Decomposition

## Conclusion

## References
